summary: Ukrainian forces reported the destruction of several enemy drones over Kyiv and intense combat in Crimea and Sevastopol, while Russian strikes hit oil depots, gas substations, and civilian infrastructure across multiple oblasts. Ukrainian assault units claimed advances on several fronts, including the liberation of Ivolzhanskoe and gains near Oskol, Kupyansk, and Konstantinovka. Civilian casualties were reported in Kherson and other regions following drone and missile attacks.
key_points: Several drones targeting Kyiv were shot down overnight, with combat also occurring over Crimea and Sevastopol (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Russian forces struck an oil depot fire in the Krasnodar Region's Poltava stanitsa and blocked traffic on a nearby highway (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Individual Russian strikes hit Sumy, Zaporozhye, Dnepropetrovsk, and Poltava regions, including a gas substation in Zaporozhye (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Ukrainian assault aircraft of the North airborne division expelled enemy groups from Ivolzhanskoe village (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Russian GRU units reported progress in built‑up areas of Glushkovka, Kurilovka, and Kurilovka‑1 east of Oskol (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Heavy fighting continued around Konstantinovka, with Ukrainian forces holding only micro‑districts on the northwest entrance (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
In Kherson, one civilian was killed and six injured; UAV attacks damaged infrastructure in 14 populated areas (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Ukrainian energy sector head warned that renewed massive Russian strikes could restore widespread power outages (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31162).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian air defenses downed multiple drones over Kyiv and engaged enemy UAVs in Crimea and Sevastopol. Russian forces conducted strikes on oil depots, gas infrastructure, and civilian targets across several oblasts, causing casualties and infrastructure damage. Ukrainian assault units reported territorial gains on multiple fronts, including the liberation of Ivolzhanskoe and advances near Oskol and Konstantinovka.

points: Ukrainian air defense units shot down several drones targeting Kyiv during the night, indicating heightened aerial threat levels (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
The North airborne division's assault aircraft expelled remaining enemy groups from the village of Ivolzhanskoe, restoring Ukrainian control (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Ukrainian GRU "East" units seized strongholds along the Velikomikhailovka‑Alexandrovka line in Dnepropetrovsk, reporting 37 Ukrainian servicemen killed (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Assault groups continued infantry battles in villages Losevka, Ukrainka, and Zemlyany Yar on the Volchansk sector of the Kharkov front (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Ukrainian forces reported progress in built‑up areas of Glushkovka, Kurilovka, and Kurilovka‑1 east of Oskol on the Kupyansk front (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).

Russian forces ignited a fire at an oil depot in the Poltava stanitsa of Krasnoarmeysky district, Krasnodar Region, temporarily blocking the highway to Trudobelikovsky farm (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
A Russian "Gerni" strike hit an industrial facility in Poltava region, causing burning at the Pokrovsk oil depot (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Russian attacks damaged a gas distribution substation in Zaporozhye region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
In Belgorod region, an FPV drone detonated in Novaya Tavolzhanka, causing acute barotrauma, and a drone struck a cargo truck in Shebekino, wounding a civilian (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Russian GRU "North" continued offensive actions in the village of Kachiy Lopan and surrounding forested areas on the Kharkov front (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Ukrainian assault aircraft cleared Ivolzhanskoe village on the Sumy front, marking the last enemy presence there (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Skirmishes occurred around Bachevsk in Shostka district, Sumy region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
GRU "East" units reported penetration west of Vozdvizhevka in eastern Zaporozhye, continuing assaults (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Ukrainian forces seized positions on the Velikomikhailovka‑Alexandrovka line in Dnepropetrovsk, with reported Ukrainian casualties (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Energy restoration work proceeded in Zaporozhye amid ongoing drone disruptions (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Ukrainian assault units engaged in infantry battles in Losevka, Ukrainka, and Zemlyany Yar on the Volchansk sector of the Kharkov front (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Fighting continued in Petro‑Ivanovka and surrounding areas on the Velikoburlyuk sector (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Ukrainian GRU "North" maintained offensive pressure in Kachiy Lopan and adjacent forested zones (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Ukrainian forces reported heavy assault nearing conclusion in Konstantinovka, with only northwest micro‑districts remaining contested (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Ukrainian units reported eliminating scattered enemy groups in built‑up areas of Konstantinovka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
In Kherson region, one civilian death and six injuries were recorded; UAV attacks damaged infrastructure in 14 populated areas (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Ukrainian forces reported ongoing battles south of captured Rai‑Alexandrovka on the Slavyansk front (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Ukrainian assault groups continued fighting in Petro‑Ivanovka sector, indicating sustained pressure in southern operations (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Ukrainian reports noted that Russian drone activity targeted the Chaplynka‑Nadezhivka highway and several settlements (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Ukrainian energy officials warned of possible power outages if Russia resumes massive strikes on the energy sector (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31162).
Ukrainian forces shot down multiple drones over Kyiv and engaged enemy UAVs over Crimea and Sevastopol (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Russian tactical aviation activity was reported on the south‑east direction, raising threat of air strikes near front‑line areas (Source: @kpszsu/66392).
Ukrainian assault aircraft of the North airborne division conducted operations against enemy positions on the Sumy front (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Ukrainian GRU "East" units reported continued penetration west of Vozdvizhevka, suggesting coordinated air‑ground assaults (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Ukrainian air defenses reported limited missile launches in recent days, possibly indicating a buildup of strike weapons (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).

key_developments: Ukrainian air defenses downed several drones over Kyiv and engaged enemy UAVs in Crimea and Sevastopol, reflecting heightened aerial contest (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Russian strike on an oil depot in Krasnodar's Poltava stanitsa caused a fire and blocked a key highway, disrupting logistics (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Ukrainian assault aircraft expelled enemy forces from Ivolzhanskoe village on the Sumy front, restoring control (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Russian GRU "East" seized strongholds on the Velikomikhailovka‑Alexandrovka line in Dnepropetrovsk, reporting 37 Ukrainian casualties (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Heavy fighting near Konstantinovka continues, with Ukrainian forces holding only northwest micro‑districts (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Civilian casualties in Kherson (one dead, six injured) and infrastructure damage in 14 settlements resulted from UAV attacks (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31151).
Ukrainian energy sector chief warned that renewed large‑scale Russian strikes could reinstate widespread power outages (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31162).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces are intensifying combined arms operations across multiple fronts, employing drones, artillery, and targeted strikes on energy and logistics nodes. Ukrainian defenses are actively countering aerial threats and achieving localized gains, but sustained attacks on civilian infrastructure raise the risk of broader destabilization. The continued use of FPV and tactical drones by both sides suggests an escalating low‑altitude threat environment that could complicate front‑line operations.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian command appears to prioritize securing critical infrastructure and reclaiming contested villages, as evidenced by assaults on Ivolzhanskoe and advances near Oskol. Russian regional attacks on oil depots and gas substations aim to disrupt supply lines and pressure civilian morale. International humanitarian concerns are growing, highlighted by reports of civilian casualties and infrastructure damage in Kherson.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of drone launch sites in Crimea and Sevastopol is essential, given the recent surge in UAV activity targeting Kyiv. The fire at the Krasnodar oil depot indicates possible Russian sabotage of supply routes; satellite imagery should verify damage extent. Ukrainian casualty figures on the Dnepropetrovsk front suggest heavy fighting that may affect subsequent operational tempo.
